rationale: Bake-off 2026-05-13 (kstrat2001/darkmux#45 closure). D (qwen3.6-35b-a3b-turboquant-mlx) hired for routine coding — won 4/4 paired rounds. B (qwen3-coder-next-mlx) reserved for single-shot prose quality (voice-editor was the Pair 2 Taste 5/5 standout). A (qwen3.5-122b-a10b) reserved as heavy-reasoning defensive — not pinned to any role; operators escalate manually. mission-compiler promoted to D 2026-05-19 (#203): the original 4B admin pinning followed the bounded-structuring framing from CLAUDE.md Beat 21, but the FinHub SYS-2476 dispatch (Beat 30) made the architectural mismatch visceral — mission planning is judgment-bearing strategy work that sets the shape for every downstream dispatch, not bounded structuring. The 4B model under-decomposes by default (returned 1 sprint for clearly multi-tree work); the fan-out compounds — bad decomposition → oversized sprint scope → first-try dispatch fails on scope persistence → recovery cycles all the way down. Mission planning belongs at the specialist tier, even at the cost of slower per-propose wall time (infrequent operation, not throughput-sensitive). Non-SWE roles default to D until per-role bake-offs land for them.
